_note: Battle of Nanjun (siege of Jiangling); Zhuge Liang seizes Jingzhou, Xiangyang, and Nanjun while Zhou Yu fights Cao Ren. Winter 208 into 209.
Zhuge Liang negotiates with Lu Su over Jingzhou; Zhao Zilong takes Guiyang; the four southern commanderies fall to Liu Bei.
Guan Yu releases Huang Zhong at Changsha; Sun Quan's first Hefei campaign vs Zhang Liao.
Sun Quan's sister married to Liu Bei (autumn 209 per Sanguozhi). Dowager Wu approves; Zhou Yu's marriage-trap fails.
Lady Sun and Liu Bei escape Wu and return to Jingzhou; Zhuge Liang humiliates Zhou Yu a second time.
Bronze Bird Tower banquet (completed 210); Zhuge Liang's third provocation of Zhou Yu over the proposed 'borrow a road to take Shu' ploy.
Zhou Yu dies at Baqiu aged 36 (autumn 210); Pang Tong recommended to Liu Bei via Lu Su's letter.
Ma Teng killed at Xuchang (Cao Cao's trap, June 211); Ma Chao raises Xiliang and marches east — opening of the Tong Pass campaign.
Xu Chu's stripped duel with Ma Chao; text explicitly cites 'ninth month of the sixteenth year of Rebuilt Tranquillity (AD 211)'. Cao Cao's letter sows discord between Han Sui and Ma Chao.
Zhang Song's embassy to Cao Cao rebuffed (late 211); he turns to Liu Bei carrying the maps of Yi Province; Pang Tong urges occupation of Shu.
